title: TC001-Create a patient session and land on the dashboard
description: Verifies a user can register a new patient profile and is taken to the main dashboard with the persisted profile visible.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/register to reach the registration form (explicit navigation as requested).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Test Patient')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should show the registered patient Test Patient after registration"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The registration flow could not be tested because the web app did not load and the registration page is blank.

Observations:
- The registration page returned a blank/empty page.
- The page shows 0 interactive elements (no form fields or buttons).
- I navigated to /register but the SPA did not initialize.

title: TC002-Generate speech from a selected phrase and play audio
description: Verifies that selecting a phrase triggers voice synthesis and results in audible playback being started in the UI.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Try waiting briefly for the SPA to render; if nothing appears, navigate directly to the communication route (/communication) to reach the patient communication UI.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/communication")
        
        # -> Make one final navigation attempt to the app root (http://localhost:5173) to try to load the SPA. If the page remains blank after this attempt, stop and report the test as BLOCKED because the communication UI cannot be reached.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Playing')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The audio playback control should indicate the phrase is Playing after selection."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Start gaze communication after successful calibration and tracking
description: Verifies that a patient can complete gaze calibration, start tracking, and see the communication UI respond to a quadrant selection with a phrase choice available.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Fill the patient registration form with the provided patient data and scroll to reveal the Start Session control (then submit if it appears).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Gaze Happy')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('29')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ALS Stage 2')
        
        # -> Fill the remaining registration fields (Room/Bed, Caregiver Name, Caregiver Phone) and scroll to reveal the Start Session control so it can be clicked next.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ICU-7')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[5]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Dr. Priya Sharma')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[6]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('+911234567890')
        
        # -> Fill all registration fields again and submit the form (trigger Start Session). After submission, wait for calibration UI to appear so calibration can be started.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Gaze Happy')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('29')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ALS Stage 2')
        
        # -> Wait for the SPA to finish loading and re-check the page for interactive elements; if the page remains empty, navigate directly to /register to reach the Patient Registration form.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # -> Reload/recover the SPA by navigating to the app root (http://localhost:5173) and wait for the page to finish loading so interactive elements appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /register and wait for the SPA to finish loading so the registration form and Start Session controls become available.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # -> Recover the SPA by navigating to the app root (http://localhost:5173) and waiting for it to finish loading so interactive elements appear. If still blank, try /register next.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Recover the SPA by navigating to /register and wait for the page to finish loading so interactive elements (registration form / Start Session) appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Choose a phrase')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The communication dashboard should present phrase choices after selecting a quadrant."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The single-page app failed to render so the registration, calibration, and tracking UIs could not be reached or exercised.

Observations:
- The /register page is blank and shows 0 interactive elements.
- Multiple navigation (root: 3 attempts, /register: 2 attempts) and wait attempts did not recover the UI.

title: TC004-Rehydrate a previously created session after browser reload
description: Verifies that after registering a patient, reloading the page restores the same patient session and shows the patient profile without re-registering.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to the registration page at http://localhost:5173/register so the registration form can be used.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # -> Reload the /register page to try to get the SPA to load, then wait for the UI to render.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Rehydrate Patient')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The patient profile for Rehydrate Patient should be visible after reload to confirm the same session was restored"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC006-Queue speech request when voice synthesis is unauthorized
description: Verifies that when voice synthesis cannot be authorized, the app queues the speech request and keeps the communication UI usable without crashing.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Fill the Patient Registration form fields and scroll to reveal the 'Start Session' button so we can begin the communication session.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Test Patient')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('45')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[3]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ALS Stage 2')
        
        # -> Fill the remaining registration fields (Room/Bed, Caregiver Name, Caregiver Phone) and scroll down to reveal the 'Start Session' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[4]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('ICU-7')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[5]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Dr. Priya Sharma')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[6]/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('+919876543210')
        
        # -> Reload the app (force a full page load) to restore the SPA and reveal interactive elements so I can continue to the communication UI.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/?reload=1")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Speech queued')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The speech request should display 'Speech queued' indicating it was queued for retry when voice synthesis could not be authorized.",
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Select a phrase')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The communication UI should remain available allowing the user to Select a phrase for further interaction.",
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173/register and observe the registration form (fields, required markers). If the route doesn't exist or the page remains blank, report the feature as missing and finish.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/register")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'This field is required')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The registration form should show a validation error indicating the required field is missing."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
